subject: Mathematics
title: Map Projections
description: A micro-lesson that makes Map Projections usable.

hook.text: Flatten a globe onto paper and something must distort. Every map lies about something.

buildup.text: The Mercator projection preserves angles but makes Greenland look bigger than Africa.

discovery.text: This happens because spherical geometry can't map to a flat plane without stretching.

twist.text: No perfect flat map exists. Mathematicians proved this is geometrically impossible.

climax.text: Every map is a compromise—shape, area, distance, or direction must sacrifice something.

punchline.text: Every flat map is a beautiful geometric lie.

quiz.question: Why can't a flat map perfectly represent the Earth?
options: A sphere's surface can't map to a plane without distortion
Maps are always drawn too small
